name: Cinnamon Shortbreads
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 170°C (325°F, Gas Mark 3). Grease a shallow baking tin measuring 19 cm x 29 cm (7-1/2 inch x 11-1/2 inch) with butter.
2. In a large bowl or food processor, sift together the flours, semolina or ground rice, and cinnamon. Add any bran residue from the sieve.
3. Add the butter or margarine and caster sugar. Mix the ingredients until they form a soft dough that leaves the sides of the bowl clean.
4. Press the dough into the prepared tin, leveling the surface with the back of a metal spoon. Prick the surface all over with a fork.
5. Bake for about 45 minutes, or until the shortbread is set and crispy on top with a light golden color. Baking time may be shorter due to the initial brownness.
6. To check if the shortbread is done, lift an end piece with a spatula and inspect the underside. If it looks cooked, cut the remaining pieces into sections and let them cool in the tin.
7. Store any unused shortbread in the tin for several days or freeze for later use. The frozen shortbread can be used almost straight from the freezer.
